description_long: This is a large, rectangular office building with a modern, utilitarian design, featuring a grid-like facade of evenly spaced vertical columns and horizontal rows of windows. The exterior is clad in light gray concrete or stone panels, with blue-tinted glass windows arranged in a highly regular, repetitive pattern across all visible sides. The building has a flat roof with a series of raised vertical fins or parapets along the roofline, adding a sense of rhythm and verticality. There are several attached structures at the base, including a prominent main entrance with a canopy and a secondary, lower annex with additional windows. The rear and side elevations show service entrances and loading docks, indicating functional access points. The overall appearance is imposing and institutional, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century office architecture in urban environments.
description: A large, rectangular office building with a grid of blue-tinted windows, light gray concrete panels, and a flat roof with vertical fins. The facade is highly regular, with a prominent main entrance and attached annexes.
description_view_0: This view shows a corner perspective with the tall, windowed facade and a side annex, highlighting the vertical fins and the main entrance canopy.
description_view_1: The front view displays the full width of the building, emphasizing the symmetrical window grid, vertical fins, and the main entrance with a canopy at the center.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the opposite corner, showing the rear annex, service entrances, and the continuation of the window pattern along the side.
description_view_3: The rear view features the back annex with additional windows, loading docks, and the regular window grid, with the vertical fins visible along the roofline.
attribute_window_pattern: Uniform vertical and horizontal grid of rectangular windows, evenly spaced in rows and columns across all facades.
